subject: two rivals forced to dance together
lyrics:
[Verse]
You stepped on my name like it was gum on the floor
Now the DJ's got us locked, can't run for the door
Your hand on my waist like a knife in a glove
We hate the same beat, but the beat doesn't love
Tu mirada me reta, I answer with hip
Every turn is a war we pretend is a trip
[Chorus]
Baila, baila, enemies in the heat
Two crowns colliding on the same broken street
I lead, you push, neither one gonna fall
The whole barrio watching us settle it all
[Verse]
Drums like a heartbeat I'd rather not share
Sweat on your collar, salt in the air
You whisper a threat, I laugh in the spin
Losing this dance means I never get in
[Bridge]
Maybe the crown was a lie all along
Maybe the only truth left is the song
My rhythm in yours, your fire in mine
We stopped keeping score around midnight, on time
[Chorus]
Baila, baila, enemies in the heat
Two crowns colliding on the same broken street
No flag, no winner, just bass in the wall
The whole barrio watching us give them it all

subject: coming home from the war
lyrics:
[Verse]
I carried my boots through the valley of smoke
Counted my brothers, then counted the folk
The river was muddy, the bridge was on fire
But a voice in the dark kept lifting me higher
Mama's old porch light I saw in my sleep
A promise too heavy for soldiers to keep
[Chorus]
I'm coming home, hallelujah, I'm coming home
The Lord walked the trenches, I never walked alone
Lay down the rifle, lay down the years
Wash these tired hands in the well of my tears
[Verse]
The medals are heavy, the silence is more
I buried young men I'll answer for
But grace met me there in the bend of the road
And took half the weight of this terrible load
[Bridge]
Sing it, choir, sing it loud
For every name carved in that crowd
The gates swung open, the bell started to ring
And a beaten old soldier remembered to sing
[Chorus]
I'm coming home, hallelujah, I'm coming home
The Lord walked the trenches, I never walked alone
Lay down the burden, lay down the war
My God was waiting at my mother's front door

subject: a barber who knows the whole town
lyrics:
[Verse]
The bell over his door is a half-remembered hymn
Dust in the sunbeam, the mirror going dim
He knows your grandfather's part, the way it used to fall
He keeps every secret in the cracks of the wall
Scissors like soft rain, hush against your ears
He's been trimming this town for forty floating years
[Chorus]
Under the slow fan, time goes liquid and gold
He holds the whole village in stories untold
A chair and a razor, a window, the light
The town comes apart and he stitches it tight
[Verse]
The widow, the mayor, the boy who left and came back
They all sit and confess to the snap of his pack
He never repeats it, just nods and he hums
And dusts off your collar when the quiet one comes
[Bridge]
When he finally closes, the lock turns so slow
A hundred reflections with nowhere to go
[Chorus]
Under the slow fan, time goes liquid and gold
He holds the whole village in stories untold
A chair and a razor, a window, the light
The town comes apart and he stitches it tight

subject: watching your father grow old
lyrics:
[Verse]
The ladder he climbed every spring now leans in the shed
He asks me the year and I lie a little instead
His hands built the table we're all sitting around
Now they shake when he reaches, they can't hold the sound
[Chorus]
And the light gets longer, the shadow gets thin
The man who was mountains is letting me in
I hold the same hands that once carried me high
We watch the same window, we don't have to try
[Verse]
He forgets where he parked but remembers the war
Remembers my mother coming through the kitchen door
The stories loop softer, the kettle goes cold
I'm learning the slow grammar of growing old
[Bridge]
No words, just the swell, just the cymbals that climb
The whole room dissolving in the long fold of time
I press my hand flat on his back as he breathes
And the guitars open up like the wind through the leaves
[Chorus]
And the light gets longer, the shadow gets thin
The man who was mountains is letting me in
I hold the same hands that once carried me high
We watch the same window. We don't say goodbye

subject: a factory closing down
lyrics:
[Verse]
The whistle that woke us won't sing anymore
They chained up the gate and they bolted the door
My father's whole life in that furnace's glow
Now rust eats the rail where the railcars don't go
The crow on the smokestack has nothing to say
The last shift walked out in the gray light of day
[Chorus]
Lord, the town's a coffin and the lid's coming down
The river runs black through a forgotten town
No bread in the oven, no fire in the steel
Just the ghost of a wage and a debt we still feel
[Verse]
The diner went dark and the church bell went still
The young ones are leaving on the road past the hill
I keep my old badge in a drawer by the bed
Like a coin for the ferry, like words for the dead
[Bridge]
Who buys the houses? Who feeds the dogs?
Who answers the men sitting still as the logs?
The machines are all silent, the silence is loud
And we wear our last paycheck like a funeral shroud
[Chorus]
Lord, the town's a coffin and the lid's coming down
The river runs black through a forgotten town
No bread in the oven, no fire in the steel
Just the ghost of a wage and a debt we still feel

subject: a New Orleans funeral
lyrics:
[Verse]
They carried him slow down Tremé in the heat
A tuba and a tear keeping time with our feet
Umbrellas like flowers turned up to the sun
We cried for the start, then we danced for the done
Grandma in white with a handkerchief raised
Singing him out through the hush and the haze
[Chorus]
So strike up the brass, let the second line roll
We give him to heaven, we keep him in soul
Death came knocking, but joy answered loud
We bury our sorrow in the sway of the crowd
[Verse]
The trumpet bent notes that the preacher couldn't say
The whole avenue moving in a gold-dusted way
A dirge turning sweet as the trombone broke free
And he danced one more time in the memory of me
[Bridge]
Sweat and the spirit, the snare and the prayer
A city that grieves like nobody else dares
[Chorus]
So strike up the brass, let the second line roll
We give him to heaven, we keep him in soul
Death came knocking, but joy answered loud
We bury our sorrow in the sway of the crowd

subject: two rivals forced to dance together
lyrics:
[Verse]
No drum tonight, only breath in the room
Two enemies floating in a borrowed perfume
Your shoulder a wall I have studied for years
Now warm under fingers, dissolving the years
The synths come like fog off a slow-moving sea
And the hatred unspools into something set free
[Chorus]
We drift, we drift, no winner, no end
The rival becomes an unlikely friend
Slow as the tide pulling sand from the shore
We forget what we held all our weapons for
[Verse]
A pad like a heartbeat too quiet to name
We move and we mirror, we let go of the game
The silence between us is wider than war
And softer than anything either swore
[Bridge]
Hold. Just hold. Let the reverb decide
Which half of this stillness is yours, which is mine
[Chorus]
We drift, we drift, no winner, no end
The rival becomes an unlikely friend
Slow as the tide pulling sand from the shore
We forget what we held all our weapons for

subject: a recurring dream of flying
lyrics:
[Verse]
Every night the same field, the same fence I climb over
Then the dirt lets me go and I'm up past the clover
The church spire shrinks to a nail in the green
And I see all the roads that my waking has been
No wings, just a will, and the wind takes me higher
Over the river, the dead, and the wire
[Chorus]
Oh I fly, I fly till the rooster's first cry
Then the floorboards reach up and they pull from the sky
The blanket, the ceiling, the cold morning air
And I'm chained to the bed with the dream still right there
[Verse]
I've told no one this, they would salt up my name
Say the Devil's got hold of a man flown insane
But up there I'm pardoned, up there I am clean
The only true country my waking has seen
[Bridge]
One night I won't land, one night I'll go on
Past the last hollow holler, gone before dawn
[Chorus]
Oh I fly, I fly till the rooster's first cry
Then the floorboards reach up and they pull from the sky
The blanket, the ceiling, the cold morning air
And I'm chained to the bed with the dream still right there

subject: the last night of a festival
lyrics:
[Verse]
Dust on the speakers, the moon hanging low
Last night of the season, nobody wants to go
The pepper soup steaming, the generators hum
We dey count down the hours till the morning come
Your gele still standing though your shoes are long gone
We go dance this thing till the breaking of dawn
[Chorus]
One more time, one more rhythm, hold am tight
Na the last, last, last of the festival night
Log drum dey talk and the bassline reply
We no go sleep till we greet the sky
[Verse]
The vendor selling suya by the flickering flame
Strangers from far places now we know your name
The DJ refuse make the final song play
'Cause the final song mean we waka away
[Bridge]
When the sun touch the canvas and the tents come down
We carry this fire back to every town
[Chorus]
One more time, one more rhythm, hold am tight
Na the last, last, last of the festival night
Log drum dey talk and the bassline reply
We no go sleep till we greet the sky

subject: looking back at your first open mic
lyrics:
[Verse]
I was eighteen and shaking with a borrowed guitar
The room was half empty, the lights were too far
My voice cracked like coconut dropped on the stone
But I sang my one song and I sang it alone
The barman just nodded, a girl tapped her glass
And something inside me decided to last
[Chorus]
Oh, the first time I stood in the light
Didn't know wrong from the right, just the song in the night
Now I look back down the long, dusty lane
And I'd shake for that mic all over again
[Verse]
Three people clapping like the sound was brand new
I forgot the third verse so I made one up too
The fear and the fire, the smell of the bar
The long walk back home with my heart and guitar
[Bridge]
Every big stage that I stood on since then
Is just that small corner, again and again
[Chorus]
Oh, the first time I stood in the light
Didn't know wrong from the right, just the song in the night
Now I look back down the long, dusty lane
And I'd shake for that mic all over again

subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
lyrics:
[Verse]
The door didn't slam but the silence sure did
We sit at the table like a couple of kids
The clock on the wall counting each careful breath
We danced all those years, now we're scared of a step
Your coffee's gone cold and my pride's gone colder
The night leaning soft on each stubborn shoulder
[Chorus]
Oh, the hush after thunder, the calm after rain
Two fools at a standstill too tender for blame
I'd give every clever, sharp word that I said
For your hand sliding over to mine instead
[Verse]
The band in my chest plays a tune nice and low
A brush on the snare, a trombone going slow
You hum the old song that we danced to in spring
And the quiet starts swinging, a soft, fragile thing
[Bridge]
No winners in love, just a beat we can keep
So I take off the gloves and I'm in over my deep
[Chorus]
Oh, the hush after thunder, the calm after rain
Two fools at a standstill too tender for blame
I'd give every clever, sharp word that I said
For your hand sliding over to mine instead

subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
lyrics:
[Verse]
Old Elias keeps a needle quicker than the train
Threading through the morning, threading out the rain
He cuffed the preacher's trousers, hemmed the bride's white lace
There's a stitch of his in nearly every Sunday face
Chalk dust on his fingers, pins between his teeth
He knows every shoulder on this crowded little street
[Chorus]
So run that banjo, let the fiddle saw
Elias dresses everybody, rich and raw
From the cradle to the coffin, from the work to the dance
There ain't a soul on the block he didn't give a second chance
[Verse]
He let out the seams when the harvest came strong
Took 'em back in again when the winter ran long
He never names the debt of the man who can't pay
Just says wear it in good health and he sends him away
[Bridge]
When they laid him to rest in the suit he had sewn
The whole block stood dressed in the cloth he had known
[Chorus]
So run that banjo, let the fiddle saw
Elias dresses everybody, rich and raw
From the cradle to the coffin, from the work to the dance
There ain't a soul on the block he didn't give a second chance

subject: looking back at your first open mic
lyrics:
[Verse]
There's a coffee ring stain on the page where I wrote
The trembling first verse and the wobbling first note
A Tuesday in autumn, a room above a bar
My whole life zipped up in a secondhand guitar
I signed the list seventh in a careful small hand
And waited three hours for the nerve I couldn't stand
[Chorus]
And I'd give a year just to stand there once more
With no one expecting a thing from the door
When the song was the song and the fear was the fuel
And I didn't know yet how the rooms could grow cruel
[Verse]
The man before me read a poem of his cat
The woman after juggled and that was that
I played and I trembled and I missed my own cue
But a stranger near the back mouthed the words with me too
[Bridge]
Everything since has been louder and wide
But nothing's been honest as that first Tuesday night
[Chorus]
And I'd give a year just to stand there once more
With no one expecting a thing from the door
When the song was the song and the fear was the fuel
And I didn't know yet how the rooms could grow cruel

subject: a poet who won't sell out
lyrics:
[Verse]
They slid me a check with a smile and a chain
Said water down the truth, put some sugar in the pain
I tore it in half, let it fall like the snow
My pen don't bend for a label or a quote
Hi-hats rolling, 808 in my chest
But the words stay sharper than the rest of the rest
They want a hook they can sell to a brand
I write what the corner already understands
[Chorus]
Nah, I won't sell, won't water it down
Keep your gold, I'ma stay underground
The truth in my mouth worth more than the deal
They can't buy a poet who refuses to kneel
[Verse]
Manager texting, the streams looking thin
Said drop the long verses, just let the trap in
But my grandmother's voice in the back of the track
Keep it real, keep it yours, and you never look back
[Bridge]
I'd rather be broke with a notebook that's true
Than rich reading lines that some boardroom drew
[Chorus]
Nah, I won't sell, won't water it down
Keep your gold, I'ma stay underground
The truth in my mouth worth more than the deal
They can't buy a poet who refuses to kneel

subject: leaving your small town for good
lyrics:
[Verse]
The gas station sign is the last light I'll see
Neon and moths and a road pulling free
I taped up the boxes, I sold off the bed
The whole town asleep in the rearview, gone red
The water tower fading, the bridge and the creek
I rehearsed this goodbye every night for a week
[Chorus]
Drive, just drive, let the city lights climb
I'm leaving this small town one last time
The synths hit the dark like a highway of chrome
Whatever comes next, it won't call me home
[Verse]
The diner where nothing and no one would change
The friends who all promised but found that too strange
The parking lot Fridays, the same dead-end song
I loved you, I did, but I knew all along
[Bridge]
Mile marker zero, the past in the smoke
The radio crackling a promise I broke
[Chorus]
Drive, just drive, let the city lights climb
I'm leaving this small town one last time
The synths hit the dark like a highway of chrome
Whatever comes next, it won't call me home
